comments.text:
Dear sir,
Recently I submitted my reimbursement claim to TPA Ericson  for Ayurvedic treatment. I purchased Ayurvedic medicines as per doctor advise from 2 ayurvedic shops. I submitted these bills to TPA alongwith other required papers. TPA raised query advising that there is no GST No. printed on bills of ayurvedic medicine shops and informed me to obtain GST No., if available. If no GST No. available, suitable declaration from shops concern with Bank stamp. I approached these 2 shops and obtain a letter/declaration that tbey do not have GST No. as their turnover is within limits prescribed by Govt. Their banker are not willing to sign these declaration. I forwarded these declarations to TPA with a specific reasons as to why TPA wants bank stamp on declaration of medical shops as the Banker wants to know purpose of putting Bank stamp on these declaratios. TPA is not answering my queries and also not conveying its decision on my claim of reimbursement. How long I have to wait for TPA decision and what is the time limit for TPA to convey its decision on claim submitted. What next course of action available to me
My doubts
1) Is it duty of patient to see that medical shop owner has GST registration or not
2) why TPA wants Bank stamp on declaration submitted by 2 shops declaring no GST No. available.
3) Should I approach Insurance company, if no decision is given by TPA say within a month
Thanks 
Satish Gujrathi
comments.author: @satishgujrathi9551

comments.text:
Sir meri wife ka health insurance claim reject ho gaya hai..

:1) Patient presented with complaints and got directly admitted to hospital without any prior OPD treatment. Also no
any post discharge follow up treatment taken.2) Patient allegedly suffering from Fever yet patient was not evaluated for notifiable
diseases as per government standard protocols.3) Higher antibiotics used without any OPD treatment and no culture sensitivity
test.4) IV antipyretics were given round the clock throughout admission irrespective of temperature chart.5) Complaints and treat ment given during hospitalization does not follow standard protocols for patient care. Hence, we regret to inform that your claim
stands repudiated in view of misrepresentation of facts.
